description: Living natural person (excludes legal persons & deceased).
Datum is about the actor — anchor for personal-data classification.
Datum permits direct or indirect identification of the actor.
Datum belongs to an Art 9(1) special-data category.
Datum relates to criminal convictions and offences.
Scenario is a processing operation (collection, storage, use, etc.).
Processing is wholly or partly carried out by automated means.
Non-automated processing forming part of a structured filing system.
Actor determines purposes and means of processing.
Actor processes personal data on behalf of the controller.
Actor develops or has developed an AI system and places it on the EU market or puts into service under its own name.
Actor uses an AI system under its authority (other than personal non-professional use).
Actor located in the Union places on the EU market an AI system bearing a third-country provider's name.
Actor in the supply chain (other than provider/importer) makes an AI system available on the market.
Product manufacturer placing on the EU market a product with an AI system as a safety component (Art 25(3)).
Actor established in the Union mandated to act on behalf of a non-EU provider.
Actor has a stable establishment in the named jurisdiction.
Actor is physically located in the named jurisdiction.
Processing carried out in the context of the activities of the actor's EU establishment, regardless of where processing physically occurs.
Scenario offers goods or services to data subjects in the Union (regardless of payment).
Scenario monitors data-subject behaviour taking place within the Union.
Actor is subject to a Member State's law by virtue of public international law.
Actor places (first makes available) the AI system on the EU market.
Actor puts the AI system into service in the EU (first use for intended purpose).
AI system output is used in the Union (relevant when provider/deployer is outside the EU).
AI system made available on the market in the named jurisdiction.
Manufacturer places on the EU market a product containing the AI system under their own name.
Target carries a closed-vocabulary feature tag (machine_based, social_scoring, compute_above_10e25, ...).
Target has a capability tag (autonomous_operation, inference_from_input). Anchors the Art 3(1) AI-system definition.
AI system is used in an Annex III use-case category.
AI system is intended to be used as a safety component of the named product.
Product (or AI system) requires third-party conformity assessment under that Annex I legislation.
Scenario falls outside the scope of Union law.
Scenario is a purely personal or household activity.
Processing by competent authorities for law-enforcement purposes (Directive 2016/680).
Processing by Union institutions/bodies (Regulation 2018/1725 governs instead).
AI system used exclusively for military or defence purposes.
AI system used exclusively for national-security purposes.
AI system specifically developed and put into service for scientific R&D only.
Research, testing, development of AI system before being placed on market or put into service.
AI system released under free and open-source licence (subject to high-risk / prohibited carve-outs).
Marker: scenario covered by lex specialis under Directive 2002/58/EC (ePrivacy). Coordination, not exclusion.
Listed Union harmonisation legislation (Annex I, Sections A & B).
Annex III high-risk use-case category.
Date a regulation entered into force.
Staggered application date for a specific provision/chapter of a regulation.
Transitional grace deadline for systems pre-dating the regulation.
Derived: datum identifies/relates to a natural person OR is special-category OR is criminal data.
Derived: datum has a category tag in the Art 9(1) closed list.
Derived: scenario is an AI system per Art 3(1) feature/capability conjunction.
Derived: scenario is a general-purpose AI model.
Derived: GPAI model with systemic risk (compute > 10^25 FLOPs OR Commission designation).
Derived: AI system uses any of the prohibited practices Art 5(1)(a)-(h).
Derived: high-risk via safety-component limb (6(1)) OR Annex III use-case limb (6(2)).
Derived: GDPR material-scope test (automated-means OR filing-system limb).
Derived: AI Act material scope (ai_system OR gpai_model).
Derived: actor anchors GDPR territorial scope (Art 3 limbs).
Derived: actor anchors AI Act territorial scope per Art 2(1)(a)-(f).
Derived: any GDPR exclusion limb fires.
Derived: any AI Act exclusion limb fires.
Derived: regulation has entered into force at the named time.
Derived: a specific provision is applicable at the named time.
Top-level: regulation governs scenario at time (in_force AND territorial AND material AND NOT excluded).
Top-level with provenance of which actor anchored territorial scope.
Scenario processes or otherwise includes the datum; bridge from processing operation to data classification.
Actor has a triggered duty under a provision for the scenario or AI system.
Actor is subject to a triggered prohibition under a provision.
The provision removes or negates a duty for the actor in the scenario.
Specific territorial limb where GDPR Article 3(2) applies; used by Article 27 representative obligations.
Article 27(2) exemption from the Article 27(1) representative designation obligation.
Processing is occasional for Article 27(2)(a).
Processing includes large-scale Article 9(1) special-category data.
Processing includes large-scale Article 10 criminal conviction/offence data.
Actor is a public authority or body for Article 27(2)(b).
Criminal-offence-data processing is carried out under control of official authority.
Union or Member State law authorises Article 10 criminal-offence-data processing with safeguards.
One of the Article 9(2) exceptions to the Article 9(1) special-category-data prohibition applies.
Data subject gave explicit consent for one or more specified special-category-data purposes.
Processing is necessary for employment, social security, or social protection law obligations/rights.
Processing is necessary to protect vital interests where the data subject is physically or legally incapable of consent.
Non-profit body processes within legitimate activities with appropriate safeguards.
Processing relates to data manifestly made public by the data subject.
Processing is necessary for legal claims or courts acting in judicial capacity.
Processing is necessary for substantial public interest on Union or Member State law basis with safeguards.
Processing is necessary for occupational/preventive medicine, medical diagnosis, health/social care, or management under law/contract.
Processing is necessary for public-health reasons under Union or Member State law with suitable measures.
Processing is necessary for archiving, scientific/historical research, or statistical purposes under Article 89(1) and law.
Controller purposes do not or no longer require identification of the data subject.
Controller can demonstrate it is not in a position to identify the data subject.
It is possible to inform the data subject.
Provider considers an Annex III AI system not to be high-risk.
AI system is intended to interact directly with natural persons.
It is obvious from the point of view of a reasonably well-informed, observant and circumspect natural person that they are interacting with AI.
AI system generates synthetic audio, image, video, or text content.
AI system is an emotion recognition system.
AI system is a biometric categorisation system.
AI system generates or manipulates image, audio, or video content constituting a deep fake.
Deep-fake disclosure exception applies, e.g. authorised by law or covered by freedom of expression/art/science with safeguards.
AI system generates or manipulates text published to inform the public on matters of public interest.
AI-generated text has undergone human review/editorial control with editorial responsibility.
GPAI model meets Article 51(1)(a) high-impact-capability condition.
Real-time remote biometric identification falls within Article 5(1)(h) law-enforcement exception and necessity limits.
Predictive policing system is used only to support human assessment based on objective and verifiable facts directly linked to criminal activity.
Emotion inference in workplace/education is intended for medical or safety reasons.
Biometric categorisation is ancillary to lawful acquired biometric datasets, such as labelling/filtering, and does not infer protected categories.
Maps a legal text row to its regulation.
Stores whether a row is recital, article, paragraph, point, or subpoint.
Stores the workbook scope tag as a Datalog fact.
Non-operative article heading/header marker.
Marks a provision as a recital; recitals are interpretive support, not direct obligations.
Links recitals to the scope dimension or interpretive topic they support.
Records the term defined by a definition row.
Links a predicate/term to the provision defining it.
Generic derived bridge for definition rows that need upstream extensional classification.
Fallback legal-effect marker where text is not safely reducible to a specific operational predicate.
Opaque upstream trigger for a provision-specific fact when text cannot be fully decomposed.
Flat permission/discretion predicate for may/empowered language.
Marks provisions that preserve or do not affect another legal instrument or more specific rule.
Article 23-style basis or safeguard marker for restrictions.
Member State activity within Chapter 2 of Title V TEU / CFSP.
Member State may/shall/more-specific-rule discretion marker.
Marks lex-specialis/no additional obligations clauses.
Legal instrument repeal date.
Reference-conversion rule after repeal/replacement.
Transitional continuation marker.
AI Act Article 6(3) factor that can support non-high-risk classification.
AI Act Article 6(3) not-high-risk classification.
Ground supporting real-time remote biometric identification exception.
Affected person located in the Union for AI Act territorial link.
Non-operative interpretive support fact, especially for recitals.
